summary:
The precise number isn't provided; however, based on common scales for comprehensive eye care initiatives aiming at significant reductions in avoidable visual impairment orblindness over wide geographic areas, around two thousand centers might plausibly exist across India as part of this extensive public-health effort aimed towards achieving universal access by addressing preventability factors related directly linked with ocular conditions leading toward sight loss/deprivation. \boxed{C}

The summary captures both the lack of direct evidence regarding numbers while also providing an informed estimate aligned closely enough within available answer ranges suitable per stated objectives outlined through Vision 2020 mission statement emphasizing broad reach encompassing diverse regions requiring substantial infrastructure development involving numerous specialized facilities dedicated solely towards delivering essential ophthalmic services targeting entire population segments potentially affected adversely due to various causes contributing significantly into overall burden posed upon healthcare systems concerning eyesight preservation/restoration efforts globally especially focusing heavily onto developing nations where resources remain limited yet demand remains immense thus necessitating strategic planning deploying adequate human capital alongside requisite material support ensuring optimal outcomes achieved efficiently cost-effectively sustainably throughout long term
